exam_price_notes: Early registration ~$600; standard registration ~$750. One-time enrollment fee $400.

testing_centers.notes: Computer-based testing at Pearson VUE centers globally. Online proctoring also available.

id_requirements: One valid, unexpired government-issued photo ID. Name must match GARP registration exactly.
exam_rules: Closed-book. On-screen calculator provided. Personal calculators not permitted. No notes or reference materials. Proctor monitors throughout.
renewal_options: FRM certification requires 40 hours of Continuing Professional Development (CPD) per year. CPD activities include professional development, education, and contribution to the risk management profession.

prerequisites.description: 2 years of relevant professional financial risk experience required to claim FRM designation (exam can be taken without this, but designation requires experience)

retake_policy.notes: FRM exams offered twice per year (May and November). No limit on total attempts. Part I and Part II must both be passed within 4 years.

official_study_resources.title: FRM Exam Study Guide
official_study_resources.notes: Official curriculum readings and practice questions available through GARP

aliases: financial risk manager frm part i
